explanation: IL3RA (CD123) is a canonical marker for human pDCs in PBMCs.
TCF4 (E2-2) is a transcription factor essential for pDC development and identity.
LILRA4 (ILT7) is highly specific for pDCs among PBMC dendritic cells.
CLEC4C (BDCA-2) is a classic surface marker for human pDCs.
SPIB is a transcription factor critical for pDC lineage specification.
IRF7 is highly expressed in pDCs, reflecting their role in type I interferon production.
JCHAIN is often upregulated in activated or mature pDCs.
MZB1 is associated with pDC activation and function.
Highly specific marker for human cDC1 subset in PBMC, distinguishing them from other DCs.
Selective for cDC1, involved in antigen cross-presentation, rarely expressed in other DC subsets.
Expressed on cDC1, used to identify and isolate this subset in human blood.
Transcription factor essential for cDC1 development and maintenance.
Transcriptional regulator required for cDC1 lineage specification.
Expressed on cDC1, supporting their identification among DCs.
Associated with cDC1 signaling pathways.
Can be upregulated in activated cDC1, though not exclusive.
Cell cycle gene, may indicate proliferation but not subset-defining.
Limited data, but reported in cDC1 transcriptomes.
Expressed in cDC1, involved in endosomal trafficking.
Enriched in cDC1, supports subset identity.
Expressed in cDC1, though also in other myeloid cells.
Reported in cDC1, though not exclusive.
Broadly myeloid, less specific but present in cDC1.
Defining marker of cDC2 (CD1c+ dendritic cells) in human blood.
Associated with CD1c+ DCs, involved in lipid antigen presentation.
Highly expressed in cDC2, distinguishing them from cDC1 and monocytes.
Selective marker for cDC2, involved in glycan recognition.
Encodes CD11c, broadly marks dendritic cells but is high in cDC2.
Expressed in myeloid lineage cells, including cDC2.
Expressed in cDC2, helps distinguish from cDC1.
Expressed in myeloid cells, including cDC2.
Low to moderate in cDC2, higher in monocytes but can be present in activated cDC2.
Similar to S100A8, can be upregulated in activated cDC2.
Expressed in myeloid cells, including cDC2.
Primarily monocyte marker, but low expression can be seen in some cDC2 subsets.
Expressed in myeloid cells, including cDC2.
Can be expressed in activated myeloid cells, including cDC2.
Expressed in myeloid cells, including cDC2.
Highly specific marker for human pDCs, distinguishing them from other DC subsets.
Encodes CD123, a canonical surface marker for pDCs in PBMC.
Transcription factor essential for pDC development and identity.
Transcription factor enriched in pDCs, involved in their differentiation.
Expressed in pDCs and involved in their function; also seen in some conventional DCs but supports pDC identity in this context.
Expressed in pDCs and can help distinguish them from other DCs.
Also known as BDCA-2, a classic pDC marker.
Expressed in pDCs, though not exclusive, supports the annotation.
